Ahmed Ali is a Sr. Network Engineer with contact information including email (ahmed.ali@email.com), phone number (+20 101 234 5678), and links to his GitHub (github.com/ahmed-ali) and LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/ahmed-ali) profiles, as well as a personal website (ahmedali.com).
Ahmed Ali received a M.Sc. in Distance Education from AISECT University between 2015 and 2017, and a B.Com. from Jiwaji University between 2009 and 2012.
The individual managed service level agreements (SLAs) and incident resolution processes, while also facilitating client meetings and performing system and hardware troubleshooting.

From 2017 to 2018, the individual orchestrated cabling, installation, commissioning, and handover processes for WiFi AP configuration in Maharashtra state railway stations for Railwire/Railtel Corporation of India Ltd. in Mumbai, and developed plans for integrating WiFi environments into station infrastructure.

From 2015 to 2016, the individual configured DPMCU, Milknet Server, EMS Server, and QMS Server infrastructure and implemented backup schedules and managed Cisco router configuration upgrades for Asha MMPC in Bali, Pali, Rajasthan.

From 2014 to 2015, the individual configured and implemented network and server infrastructure, including Windows Servers (2000/2003, 2008 R2, 2012 R2), AD, FTP, DNS, DHCP, TFTP, and Linux OS for The Scindia School in Fort, Gwalior (MP), and provided advanced troubleshooting for complex LAN environments.

From January 2017 to the present, Ahmed Ali worked as a Sr. Network Engineer at Cloudatix Network Pvt. Ltd., where he led network backup implementation and maintenance, managed Windows Servers (2008 R2, 2012 R2, 2016), and provided LAN/WAN troubleshooting.

From 2012 to 2013, the individual implemented and configured network infrastructure, including Check Point Endpoint Security MI Management Console for laptop users for M/s Teva API India Pvt Ltd in Bhind (MP), and managed over 350 Desktops and Laptops with Symantec Backup Exec 12D.
In 2019, the individual obtained a certificate in Manual and Computerized Accounting with Tally ERP9 from Tally Academy.

The individual possesses skills in various operating systems (Windows, Linux, Ubuntu, RHEL 6), network technologies (Cisco Routing & Switching, InterVLAN Routing, Dynamic Protocols, Static Routing, ACL, VTP, VLAN, EtherChannel, HSRP, STP, IPv6, LAN Troubleshooting, Structured Network Cabling, Cisco Firewall, Fortinet Firewall), networking devices (Cisco Routers, Cisco Switches, HP ProLiant Servers, Dell Servers, Lenovo Servers, Fortinet Firewalls, Modems), server technologies (Active Directory, RODC, FTP, Print Server, SCCM, WDS, DHCP, Group Policies, DNS Server, RIS Server, User & Computer Policies), and backup technologies (Server 2016 backup tools, Symantec Backup EXEC 12D), and virtualization (VMWare ESXi, VMware Workstation, Oracle VirtualBox, GNS3 Network Simulator).
From July 2015 to December 2016, Ahmed Ali was an IT Executive at Asha MMPC, where he managed network backups, administered Windows Servers, and troubleshooted LAN & WAN issues.

From April 2012 to June 2015, Ahmed Ali held positions as a Network Engineer at The Scindia School and a Network Support Engineer at M/s Teva API India Pvt Ltd, where he administered servers, managed security consoles, and provided support.
From March 2010 to March 2012, Ahmed Ali worked as a System Engineer at Nava Bharat Press (P) Ltd, managing server infrastructure and providing helpdesk support.

Nestled in the historic fort of Gwalior, Madhya Pradesh, The Scindia School stands as a venerable institution, reliant on a robust and diverse technological backbone. It’s a place where tradition meets modern necessity, evidenced by its complex network infrastructure. For years, the school has depended on a carefully managed ecosystem of Windows Servers – spanning versions from 2000/2003 through 2012 R2 – alongside the adaptable power of Linux operating systems. This foundation supports critical services like Active Directory (AD), ensuring seamless user access and security, and facilitates data transfer through FTP. The school’s digital heartbeat is maintained by meticulously configured DNS and DHCP servers, orchestrating network addresses and resolving domain names, while TFTP provides a streamlined method for network booting and updates. 

The smooth operation of this intricate system has, at times, fallen to individuals like Ahmed Ali, a Network Engineer who dedicated a period from April 2012 to June 2015 to maintaining and troubleshooting the school’s LAN environment, offering advanced support when complexities arose. He was instrumental in configuring and implementing these very systems, ensuring the school’s continued connectivity and operational efficiency. The Scindia School, therefore, isn’t simply a place of learning; it’s a digitally-supported environment, carefully constructed and maintained to serve its community.

Active Directory is the central nervous system of a Windows-dominated network, a meticulously organized directory service that governs access and resources. It’s not merely a list of users and computers; it’s the foundational framework for security, policy enforcement, and streamlined administration. Think of it as a digital gatekeeper, verifying identities and dictating permissions across an organization. 

It thrives within the ecosystem of Windows Servers – specifically the 2008 R2, 2012 R2, and 2016 iterations – acting as their core function in many deployments.  It’s a system built on hierarchies, organizing users, computers, and other network resources into logical groupings for efficient management through Group Policies.  

Those who truly understand Active Directory, like Sr. Network Engineer Ahmed Ali, see beyond the interface. They recognize its power in managing everything from user accounts and computer configurations to security settings and application deployment. Ali’s experience at Cloudatix Network and Asha MMPC demonstrates a practical mastery of its implementation, maintenance, and troubleshooting, alongside a broader skillset encompassing server administration, network infrastructure, and backup solutions. He understands that a healthy Active Directory is paramount to a stable and secure network, a silent guardian ensuring everything runs smoothly behind the scenes. It’s a system demanding precision, foresight, and a deep understanding of network principles – a responsibility Ali has consistently shouldered throughout his career.

The Cisco Router stands as a silent, steadfast workhorse of the network, a crucial node in the flow of information. Often unseen, yet perpetually active, it’s a device defined by its adaptability and the precision of its configuration. This isn’t a flashy piece of technology, but one of quiet competence, directing traffic with unwavering logic. 

Its core function is connection – bridging networks, routing data packets, and ensuring messages reach their intended destination. It’s a gatekeeper, a traffic controller, and a foundational element of modern communication. While seemingly static, the Cisco Router is anything but. It’s a dynamic entity, its internal workings constantly adjusted and refined. 

Individuals like Ahmed Ali, a Sr. Network Engineer, dedicate their expertise to its upkeep, meticulously managing its configuration, ensuring smooth operation, and implementing vital backup procedures. Ali’s work, spanning from the Asha MMPC in Bali to his current role at Cloudatix Network, highlights the router’s importance in diverse environments. He’s one of many who shape its behavior, tailoring it to specific network needs and safeguarding its functionality. 

The router doesn’t exist in isolation. It’s intrinsically linked to servers – DPMCU, Milknet, EMS, QMS – and the broader LAN/WAN infrastructure. It’s a central point of control, reliant on, and supporting, the systems around it. Its configuration, though complex, is essential for the seamless operation of these connected components. It’s a reliable, if unassuming, pillar of the digital landscape, constantly working to keep the world connected.

Symantec Backup Exec 12D is a workhorse of data protection, a reliable if somewhat dated system favored for its comprehensive capabilities. It’s a tool built for managing the digital lives of organizations, capable of safeguarding the data residing on hundreds of desktops and laptops – as evidenced by its deployment at Teva API India Pvt Ltd, where it diligently oversaw the backups of over 350 endpoints. Though newer solutions have emerged, 12D remains a testament to robust functionality, a cornerstone in the infrastructure overseen by professionals like Ahmed Ali, who skillfully administered it during his tenure. It’s a system that doesn’t demand the spotlight, but quietly and consistently ensures business continuity by preserving critical information, a silent guardian against data loss. While often paired with server environments utilizing technologies like Active Directory and various Windows Server iterations, its core strength lies in its ability to secure the individual workstations that form the front lines of any digital operation.

Ahmed Ali is a seasoned Senior Network Engineer, a meticulous troubleshooter with over a decade spent building and maintaining the digital infrastructure that keeps organizations connected. Contactable at ahmed.ali@email.com or +20 101 234 5678, and showcasing his projects online at github.com/ahmed-ali and linkedin.com/in/ahmed-ali, as well as his personal website ahmedali.com, he possesses a pragmatic blend of academic grounding and hands-on experience. 

His career began in 2010 at Nava Bharat Press, where he cut his teeth as a System Engineer, managing server infrastructure and providing essential helpdesk support. He quickly progressed, taking on roles at M/s Teva API India Pvt Ltd and The Scindia School as a Network Support Engineer and Network Engineer respectively, gaining proficiency in server administration – spanning Windows Server 2000/2003, 2008 R2, and 2012 R2 – and security console management, including Check Point Endpoint Security and CA Unicenter. 

Asha MMPC saw him step into an IT Executive role, solidifying his skills in network backup management and LAN/WAN troubleshooting. Since 2017, Ahmed has been a key player at Cloudatix Network Pvt. Ltd., where he currently leads network backup implementation and maintenance, expertly managing Windows Servers (2008 R2, 2012 R2, and 2016) and resolving complex network issues. He holds a B.Com. from Jiwaji University and furthered his education with an M.Sc. in Distance Education from AISECT University, demonstrating a commitment to continuous learning alongside a proven track record of reliable performance. He’s a practical engineer, comfortable with Cisco routers and Symantec Backup Exec, focused on keeping systems running smoothly and securely.

DHCP is a foundational element within the infrastructure expertly managed by this individual, a quietly essential service consistently deployed and maintained across diverse network environments. It’s not a flashy component, but a critical one – the silent orchestrator of IP address allocation, ensuring seamless connectivity for countless devices. From its implementation within the network backbone of The Scindia School, alongside Windows Servers spanning multiple versions (2000/2003 through 2016), to its ongoing role in maintaining stable LAN/WAN operations at Cloudatix Network Pvt. Ltd., DHCP is a constant presence in this engineer’s toolkit. 

It’s a technology he doesn’t merely *use*, but understands as integral to the larger systems he builds and troubleshoots. He’s comfortable configuring it on Windows Servers, recognizing its importance alongside related server technologies like DNS, Active Directory, and FTP.  DHCP isn’t isolated; it’s part of a cohesive network strategy, working in concert with Cisco routing and switching, firewalls from both Cisco and Fortinet, and a deep understanding of network protocols.  Its reliable function is a cornerstone of the stable, secure networks he delivers, a testament to his meticulous approach and comprehensive skillset.

FTP appears as a foundational element within a broader skillset, a reliable workhorse in the landscape of network infrastructure. It’s not a glamorous component, but a consistently present one, often residing on Windows Servers – specifically the 2008 R2, 2012 R2, and 2016 iterations. This suggests a practical, hands-on familiarity, not theoretical knowledge. 

Its presence is noted in the context of establishing and maintaining network services for institutions like The Scindia School, indicating a role in facilitating file transfer, likely for administrative or educational purposes. It’s a tool deployed alongside other core services like Active Directory, DNS, and DHCP, forming a cohesive system. 

While not always explicitly highlighted, FTP’s consistent appearance alongside server administration and network troubleshooting implies a deep understanding of its configuration, security implications, and integration within larger network ecosystems. It’s a component the individual confidently implements and maintains, a quiet but essential part of keeping data flowing.

Asha MMPC, nestled in Bali, Pali, Rajasthan, operates with a quietly efficient digital backbone. Though its core business remains undisclosed, it’s clear the organization relies heavily on robust IT infrastructure. From 2015 to 2016, a dedicated effort went into establishing and maintaining this foundation – configuring critical systems like the DPMCU, Milknet Server, EMS Server, and QMS Server, alongside diligent backup scheduling. The network itself, managed through Cisco routers, received consistent attention, undergoing necessary upgrades to ensure smooth operation. 

Asha MMPC isn’t a solitary operation, however. It’s a client of several key services – DPMCU, Milknet Server, EMS Server, and QMS Server – suggesting a reliance on specialized external support for core functions. Internally, the organization benefits from skilled IT professionals; Ahmed Ali, an IT Executive from July 2015 to December 2016, was instrumental in keeping the digital arteries flowing, expertly handling network backups, Windows Server administration, and resolving the inevitable hiccups of LAN & WAN connectivity. Asha MMPC presents as a quietly industrious entity, prioritizing a stable and well-maintained technological infrastructure to support its operations.
